top_signals.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
top_signals.summary: Brent oil slipped after surging almost 6% over the previous two sessions, as traders weighed continued hostilities between the US and Iran against efforts to broker a new ceasefire.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Reverses Gains on Renewed Hope of Peace in Iran
top_signals.summary: Crude oil prices reversed their climb this morning, retreating on reports that efforts are being made to negotiate another ceasefire between the United States and Iran. Brent crude was trading at $88.77 per barrel at the time of writing, down from over $90 per barrel, and West Texas Intermediate was changing hands for $82.31 per barrel despite continued missile strikes in the Persian Gulf and President Trump’s threat to punish Iran for the deaths of several U.S. troops. Reports about ceasefire efforts focus on negotiating a ten-day ceasefire…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover
top_signals.summary:
Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members.Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians themselves debate the issues shaping their country.Western coverage often narrows the view to the battlefield — these pieces widen it, revealing the texture of daily life, politics, and public argument in a nation at war. The perspectives gathered here are varied, candid, and often surprising, together forming a more complete picture of Ukraine as it really is.This week,

The post The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Small Business Bank and announces termination enforcement actions with BNP Paribas S.A., BNP Paribas USA, Inc., BNP Paribas Securities Corp., and Community Bankshares, Inc.

top_signals.title: London Stock Exchange plans to launch round-the-clock trading next year
top_signals.summary: New overnight venue will initially offer access to exchange-traded products

top_signals.title: U.S. imposes new 50% tariffs on $20 billion worth of Canadian products
top_signals.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump invoked Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930 in slapping import taxes on goods — the law's first known usage in nearly a century of existence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
